channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Polish town invites Elon Musk to buy its castle for his European headquarters
item.description: A town in Poland is encouraging Elon Musk to buy its vast 13th-century castle and turn it into his European headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NATO announces a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO is launching a new mission to protect undersea cables in the Baltic Sea region after a string of incidents that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the strategic region, the alliance's leader said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Zelenskyy, Macron discuss Western troop deployment in Ukraine before German defense chief visits
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that he has held further discussions with French President Emmanuel Macron about the possibility of Western troops deploying in Ukraine to safeguard any peace deal ending the nearly three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: World Economic Forum says Trump to take part virtually in Davos meeting, days after inauguration
item.description: The World Economic Forum’s president says U.S. President Donald Trump will take part virtually in the annual meeting in Davos just days after his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rescuers bring out survivors and dead from a South African mine as hundreds remain deep underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — Rescuers sent a cage-like structure down into one of South Africa’s deepest gold mines on Tuesday to bring out survivors among hundreds of miners who were working illegally in an abandoned shaft and have been trapped for months.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Malaysia is home to the world's largest hotel, with over 7K rooms for guests
item.description: The Guinness World Record for the largest hotel by number of rooms goes to First World Hotel in Malaysia. The hotel boasts over 7,000 hotel rooms for guests to lounge in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top aide of impeached South Korean president pleads for investigators to halt detention efforts
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The top aide of impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol pleaded with law enforcement agencies on Tuesday to abandon their efforts to detain him over his imposition of martial law last month, as authorities prepared a second attempt to take him into custody.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC chief prosecutor wants Israeli objections over Netanyahu warrant to be rejected
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has told judges that Israeli objections to the investigation into the 13-month war in Gaza should be rejected.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Structure discovered in Jerusalem's City of David dates back to the First Temple, study finds
item.description: Archaeologists excavating a site since 2010 uncovered an eight-room structure dating back to the First Temple in 8th century BCE, that included a wine press and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France’s new prime minister announces renegotiation of contested plan to raise the retirement age
item.description: France’s new prime minister, François Bayrou, has announced the renegotiation of a contested plan raising the retirement age from 62 to 64.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: European defense heavyweights say meeting Trump's military spending target won't be easy
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The defense ministers of Europe's five top military spenders said Monday they want to continue increasing their investments in defense but described meeting President-elect Donald Trump's challenge for them to raise spending to 5% of their overall economic output as complicated
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China reportedly building 'D-Day'-style barges as fears of Taiwan invasion rise
item.description: China is reportedly building a series of barges for amphibious landings amid fears of an invasion of Taiwan, its island neighbor, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Head of the International Court of Justice is named Lebanon's new prime minister
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new president has asked prominent diplomat and jurist Nawaf Salam to form the country’s new government after Salam was named prime minister by a large number of legislators Monday. The move apparently angered the Hezbollah group and its allies.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Gaza ceasefire talks press on in Qatar
item.description: Israel and Hamas appear to be inching closer to a phased ceasefire agreement, officials said Monday. However, a deal hasn't been reached yet and there are a number of obstacles that could still scupper the talks in Qatar. Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza began on Oct.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada ready to buy more American products to appease Trump's tariff threat, ambassador says
item.description: PALM DESERT, Calif. (AP) — Canada's ambassador to Washington said Monday that Canada is prepared to buy more from the United States, including its next fleet of submarines, to appease President-elect Donald Trump's concerns about the U.S.-Canada trade deficit.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jake Sullivan: 'We are so close' to ceasefire, hostage deal in Gaza
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan said Monday the U.S. is “so close to a ceasefire and hostage deal” in Gaza, as President Biden races to secure an agreement on a major foreign policy objective before leaving office in a week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand amid controversy, died Monday at age 82. Toscani disclosed last year he had a rare disease.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Biden delivers foreign address at State Department
item.description: President Biden began his farewell tour around Washington this week with an address on Monday at the State Department to highlight his work on foreign policy during his presidency. Biden was expected to defend his foreign policy legacy, including reconnecting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Israel, Hamas ceasefire close, but not 100 percent: Israeli official
item.description: Efforts to secure a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas and release hostages are close but not yet finalized, an Israeli official told The Hill on Monday, after Reuters reported Qatar has handed a final deal to both sides.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: 1 poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the United States. According to a poll by Patriot Polling released Sunday, 57.3 percent of respondents approve of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Suspended South Korean President Yoon to skip first day of impeachment trial over safety concerns
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol will not attend the first day of his impeachment trial this week, according to his lawyer, who cited concerns for the suspended president’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Biden admin slammed for ‘waiting’ to declare genocide in Sudan
item.description: The incoming Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee has blasted the Biden Admnin for waiting until just 13 days before their term ends to declare atrocities in Sudan’s war as genocide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poland adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz liberation commemoration
item.description: Poland passed a resolution to ensure the safe participation of Israeli representatives who attend 80th anniversary commemorations of the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation
